results.title: Robust-U1: Can MLLMs Self-Recover Corrupted Visual ... - arXiv
results.content:
## Appendix D Sensitivity Studies

This section studies how robust Robust-U1 is to three training-time design choices: (i) replacing the paired semantic reward with a reference-free alternative, (ii) varying the reward scaling factor α\alpha, and (iii) swapping the frozen semantic encoder.

### D.1 Reference-Free Semantic Reward [...] ## Appendix B Extended Quantitative Comparisons

This section reports two extended comparisons that situate Robust-U1 against alternative pipelines: (i) using state-of-the-art external restoration models as a preprocessor before a strong discriminative MLLM, and (ii) deploying recovery only when needed via a detect-then-recover variant.

### B.1 Comparison with External Restoration Modules [...] ### D.3 Sensitivity to the Choice of Semantic Encoder

Our semantic reward uses TinyCLIP (Wu et al., 2023) as the frozen encoder. To verify that our conclusions are not tied to this specific encoder, we replace TinyCLIP with three alternatives of different scales and architectures: CLIP-B/16 (Radford et al., 2021), SigLIP-B/16 (Zhai et al., 2023), and a heavily distilled, weaker CLIP (Radford et al., 2021) variant.

|  |  |  |
 --- 
| Encoder | Parameters | R-Bench Overall |
| TinyCLIP (Wu et al., 2023) (default) | 39M | 0.7398 |
| CLIP-B/16 (Radford et al., 2021) | 149M | 0.7376 (−0.30%-0.30\%) |
| SigLIP-B/16 (Zhai et al., 2023) | 150M | 0.7382 (−0.22%-0.22\%) |
| Distilled weak encoder | 9M | 0.7345 (−0.72%-0.72\%) |

results.title: BEST "Zero Waste" Multi-Color 3D Printer | XL vs H2C vs U1
results.content: it automatically grabs it and feeds it into the hotends. Makes it super easy, especially for loading and unloading filaments. You just throw them on there and they just pull them in. The unboxing and setup and calibration on this thing is fairly easy. It comes in a good amount of parts. The hotends and the wires and the little side thing, the spool holders and the filament feeders are all disassembled, but the instructions are pretty easy to follow. Everything screws together fairly easily. I want to say take you about half an hour to set this up and then you can let it run all of its calibration systems which are really easy. It just you follow the touchcreen and prompts. It comes with its own little mobile app for remote printing and monitoring and it uses a Snapmaker version of Orca. [...] very fast thanks to uh induction heating, it does have longer print times, but not by much than the tool head swappers, which can just grab the tool head and start printing immediately because it can preheat those. This can't preheat the nozzles, but it makes up for that in the fact that it doesn't also have purge per layer. It doesn't need to purge every layer and every color change, just like the tool head changer. So, it is a little bit of an oddball. It will increase your print time compared to these two. But, I think for the reliability I'm getting out of the H2C, it's worth that trade-off. It's worth a couple hours more on a print compared to risking a tool head anomaly or something with the firmware or the Prime tower falling over or needing to spend $4,000 on a printer. This thing [...] ecosystem and UI and setup is so easy to follow. It is very self-explanatory and it does all of its checks by itself right after you get it out of the box. And with the setup also comes the easy to use Bamboo app and the Bamboo Studio which is just a clone of other 3D printing softwares down the line down the years. It's super easy to use and very user friendly. The same as the U1. It has a camera AI monitoring spaghetti detection. It'll detect failures. It'll detect anomalies. It'll pause. It'll send you notification. It does all the same stuff. It's really cool. Also like time-lapse printing, which is just really awesome to see. Unlike the U1, this does have a fully heated chamber. I've been printing ASA on this. It prints it beautifully. And obviously because it has a heated chamber,

results.title: Snapmaker U1 VS Bambu Lab H2C, Coworkers or Competitor
results.content:
Next is a huge advantage of the U1 structure. The four heads not only save your time and material but also gives a better solution for soft material printing. You can print soft material in multicolor. It really helps on shoe printing, because we need different features on a shoe to make it durable, wearable and good looking. We can only do that with 2 materials and 1 color in the past. Now the game has been changed.

Let’s say you have done the enclosed thing on your Snapmaker U1, it becomes your good buddy for multi-color printing, multi-material printing, and shoe printing.

### H2C

Also starts from the cons. The H2C is heavy and huge. To get the full function, you must use stackers to arrange the AMSs, PEFE tubes, cables, and power supplies, and it takes a lot of room. [...] The printer can handle probably all of the 3D printing materials, you can print all kinds of advanced materials and make combinations as you like, as long as you use TPU for AMS for the solution of the soft material.

Since the Vortek system makes multi-color printing faster and material-saving, you can also add your H2C up to 24 colors.

Besides, if you own a laser version, laser engraving, cutting, and writing give you more possibilities on DIY.

## Price

I think we should just skip the Snapmaker U1 because we could buy 3 or 4 of it at the price of the H2C. It doesn’t even take much to add decent enclosure upgrade kit. The 849USD price make it a home use printer.

But things are much different on the Bambu Lab H2C. This printer is a studio printer. Let’s make a list for the H2C. [...] And it takes 8 hours on U1, only 1 hour faster.

Anyway, this part is physical and cannot be changed too much by an algorithm or software. The Snapmaker calls the system snapswap; it is way faster than Vortek. As for H2C, the time saving is determined by the filament group, the AMS setup, and the PEFE tube length.

## Filament Saving

Assuming that we limit the H2C to 7 colors, the basic option to use full function of Vortek system and the fastest print time it can print; Then we open the flow calibration on the U1. The U1 saves more material, because it has a smaller prime tower.

The U1 reduce the prime tower to a real tower, and the H2C uses columnar as the tower.

Besides, the filament saving is quite close in this assumed condition.

In daily uses things are quite different.

Learn more: How to Charge Your Lawn Mower Battery and Power Tools

## Riding Mower and Lawn Tractor Batteries

Riding and lawn tractor batteries play a crucial role in powering various systems essential for efficient yard, agricultural and landscaping work. These batteries provide the necessary energy for starting the engine as well as various electrical components such as hydraulic systems and running lights. Whether you’re mowing acres of land or managing a small yard, reliable battery performance ensures your equipment’s ready to work when you are.

SP-45 Lawn & Garden Battery

### SP-45 12V Riding Tractor Battery with 480 CCA

Ideal as a riding tractor battery
